_**User**_

So, I'm creating a phase 0.1 branch because I've three concerns, 

1. I would like to switch to SLNX 
2. I would like to have a centralizaed way to manage dependencies
3. Why we use Fluentvalidation 7 when 9 is out? do we have other old dependencies? 

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Good questions for a **phase 0.1** branch — these are mostly tooling hygiene, not business logic. Here is what the repo actually looks like today.

## 1. Switch to SLNX

You currently have a classic `SchaerbeekMunicipality.sln` (132 lines of GUID/platform boilerplate). There is no `.slnx` yet.

With .NET 10 (`dotnet --version` → **10.0.100**), migration is straightforward:

```bash
dotnet solution migrate
```

That generates `SchaerbeekMunicipality.slnx` — a small XML file with folders (`src/`, `tests/`) and project paths. You can then:

- Keep both during transition, or delete the `.sln` once CI/docs/README reference `.slnx`
- Update CI if anything explicitly references `*.sln` (today it runs `dotnet restore/build/test` without a solution path, so it should keep working)
- Verify IDE support (VS 2022 17.14+ / Rider recent builds handle `.slnx`)

**Recommendation for 0.1:** migrate, commit `.slnx`, remove `.sln`, update README/docs that mention the solution file.

---

## 2. Centralized dependency management

There is **no** `Directory.Packages.props` today. Every package version lives inline in each `.csproj`:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore" Version="10.0.4" />
```

You already have `Directory.Build.props` for shared MSBuild settings (nullable, warnings-as-errors, etc.), so adding **Central Package Management (CPM)** fits naturally:

**`Directory.Packages.props`** (new):

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore" Version="10.0.9" />
    <!-- one entry per package -->
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**Each `.csproj`** becomes version-less:

```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore" />
```

Benefits for this solution:

- One place to bump EF Core, Aspire, OpenTelemetry, test libs
- Easier to keep **Microsoft.* packages aligned** (you currently mix `10.0.0`, `10.0.4`, etc.)
- Natural home for future packages (FluentValidation, NSubstitute, Testcontainers) from `TECH-STACK.md`

**Recommendation for 0.1:** add CPM + normalize Microsoft/EF versions to a single patch line (e.g. `10.0.9`).

---

## 3. “FluentValidation 7” — likely **FluentAssertions**

**FluentValidation is not installed yet.** It is documented/planned for Phase 1 (`docs/phases/phase-0-foundation.md`, `TECH-STACK.md`), but no `PackageReference` exists in any project.

What **is** on v7 is **FluentAssertions 7.2.0** in both test projects — and that is **intentional**:

```252:252:docs/TECH-STACK.md
| FluentAssertions | Readable assertions — **v8+ is free only for non-commercial/OSS use**; fine for this project, or pin v7 (Apache-2.0) |
```

So it is not “we picked an old FluentValidation.” It is “we pinned FluentAssertions 7 for Apache-2.0 licensing.” Latest is **8.10.0**, which has a different license model.

When you add FluentValidation in Phase 1, you would add the current major (11.x line as of 2026 — not 9; you may have been thinking of another package). No reason to start on an old major.

### Other packages that are behind latest

From `dotnet list package --outdated`:

| Area | Current | Latest | Notes |
|------|---------|--------|-------|
| **FluentAssertions** | 7.2.0 | 8.10.0 | **Keep 7** unless you accept v8+ licensing |
| **Microsoft EF / ASP.NET** | 10.0.0–10.0.4 (mixed) | 10.0.9 | Safe patch bump; CPM helps align |
| **OpenTelemetry** (ServiceDefaults) | 1.13.x | 1.15–1.16 | Template defaults; bump when convenient |
| **Aspire** | 13.0.2 | 13.4.6 | Minor bump; test AppHost after |
| **MudBlazor** | 8.15.0 | **9.6.0** | **Major** — defer unless you want a UI migration in 0.1 |
| **coverlet.collector** | 6.0.4 | 10.0.1 | Major jump; low risk but not urgent |
| **Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk** | 17.14.1 | 18.7.0 | Major; fine on 17 for now |

Nothing looks “accidentally ancient.” Most lag is either **template pinning** (Aspire/OpenTelemetry), **intentional** (FluentAssertions 7), or **major-version caution** (MudBlazor 8→9).

---

## Suggested scope for `phase-0.1`

| Task | Risk | Value |
|------|------|-------|
| Migrate to `.slnx`, drop `.sln` | Low | Cleaner solution file |
| Add `Directory.Packages.props` + CPM | Low | Single source of truth for versions |
| Align Microsoft/EF packages to one patch | Low | Fewer subtle mismatches |
| Bump Aspire + OpenTelemetry | Low–medium | Test AppHost + `/health` |
| Keep FluentAssertions **7.x** | — | License clarity |
| Bump MudBlazor 8→9 | Medium | UI breaking changes — probably Phase 1+ |
| Add FluentValidation | — | Wait until Phase 1 slices need it |

If you want, I can implement the 0.1 branch changes (SLNX + CPM + aligned package bumps) in one pass and run build/tests to verify.

---

_**User**_

ok you are right for fluentvalidation - can you migrate slnx and do the centralized package?

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Deleting the old .sln file. Creating Directory.Packages.props for centralized package management. Updating all csproj files. AppHost SDK version set to 13.

Centralizing the Aspire AppHost SDK version is tricky because the Sdk attribute does not support MSBuild property expansion. The SDK version may instead be defined in Directory.Build.props.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The README solution layout omits the .sln file. CI restores packages without it. This is fine.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Done. Here is what changed:

### SLNX migration
- Generated `SchaerbeekMunicipality.slnx` with `src/` and `tests/` folders
- Removed the old `SchaerbeekMunicipality.sln` (132 lines → 18 lines of XML)

### Central Package Management
- Added `Directory.Packages.props` with all 24 package versions grouped by area (Aspire, Microsoft, EF Core, OpenTelemetry, UI, Testing)
- Removed inline `Version=` from every `PackageReference` in all 6 projects that use packages

Package versions are unchanged from before — this is structural only, not a bump pass.

### Verified
```bash
dotnet restore SchaerbeekMunicipality.slnx
dotnet build SchaerbeekMunicipality.slnx --configuration Release
dotnet test SchaerbeekMunicipality.slnx --configuration Release
```
All 7 projects build; 2 tests pass.

**Note:** The Aspire AppHost SDK version (`Aspire.AppHost.Sdk/13.0.2`) stays in the AppHost `.csproj` — that is SDK pinning, not a NuGet `PackageReference`, so CPM does not cover it. Keep it aligned with `Aspire.Hosting.PostgreSQL` in `Directory.Packages.props` when you bump Aspire.

CI still works as-is (`dotnet restore/build/test` without a solution path). You can optionally point it at `SchaerbeekMunicipality.slnx` for explicitness.

Want me to commit this on your phase-0.1 branch?

---

_**User**_

ok that\s perfect - you can add a document in the phases folder to speak about this

_**Agent (default)**_

Added [`docs/phases/phase-0.1-tooling-hygiene.md`](docs/phases/phase-0.1-tooling-hygiene.md) and linked it from [`docs/phases/README.md`](docs/phases/README.md).

The doc covers:

- **Summary** — tooling-only pass, no business logic changes
- **SLNX migration** — why, structure, migration command
- **Central Package Management** — how `Directory.Packages.props` works, package groups, Aspire SDK exception
- **Version policy** — FluentAssertions 7 pin, deferred bumps (MudBlazor 9, FluentValidation in Phase 1)
- **How to bump packages** — workflow + `dotnet list package --outdated`
- **Verification commands** — build/test via `.slnx`
- **Deferred items** — optional follow-ups

It follows the same structure as the Phase 0 foundation doc so the phases folder stays consistent.
